title: AI Talent Index — U.S. vs China
subtitle: Three-proxy composite: research volume, top-conference presence, and high-impact output. Each proxy scored as share-of-combined (US + China = 100). US + China composite ≈ 100 by construction.
description: A composite index measuring relative AI talent strength across three dimensions: (1) AI paper volume (breadth of research output), (2) top AI conference papers with ≥10 citations (quality of conference-level research), and (3) high-impact AI papers with ≥50 citations (elite research production). All proxies sourced from OpenAlex. Each is scored as US share of combined US+China total.

paper_volume.note: AI papers (AI/ML/NLP/CV concepts) on OpenAlex, last 365 days, grouped by author institution country. China leads on volume; US tends to lead on citation impact.

top_conference.note: AI papers published in conference proceedings (OpenAlex source type=conference) with at least 10 citations, last 730 days. Covers NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, CVPR, ACL, AAAI, IJCAI and other major AI venues without requiring venue-specific ID lookups.

high_impact.note: AI papers of any type with at least 50 citations, last 1095 days. Uses a 3-year window to give papers adequate time to accumulate citations. Captures field-defining research regardless of venue.

paper_volume.note: China leads on AI paper volume (231,714 vs US 130,881 over 365 days). OpenAlex may undercount Chinese domestic journals not indexed in major international databases.

top_conference.note: China has substantially increased top AI conference presence since 2019, particularly at NeurIPS, ICML, and CVPR. Citation thresholding filters out poster/workshop papers.

high_impact.note: US tends to lead on high-citation AI papers, reflecting concentration of frontier lab research (Google DeepMind, OpenAI, Meta FAIR, Microsoft Research) in the US ecosystem.

interpretive_sentence: On a composite of paper volume (30%), top conference presence (40%), and high-impact output (30%), the US scores 37.8 and China scores 62.2 out of 100 (US + China ≈ 100). China leads on raw paper volume (63.9% of combined); the US leads on top-conference cited papers (40.0%) and high-impact papers (36.5%).

composite_construction.method: Weighted average of three share-of-combined scores. Each proxy is computed as US/(US+China)*100, giving a score where US+China=100. Composite = 0.30*(paper_volume_share) + 0.40*(top_conference_share) + 0.30*(high_impact_share). Rationale: top-conference presence weighted highest as the most direct measure of research quality at AI-specific venues.

source.name: OpenAlex API
source.note: group_by=authorships.institutions.country_code on AI/ML/NLP/CV concepts. Papers with authors from multiple countries are counted in each country. Conference-type filter uses locations.source.type:conference.

papers.title: ChatGPT as a Tool for Programming Assistance and Code Development
The Vinness Wagyu Theorem: On the Epistemic Independence of Direct Knowledge from Formal Verification
VGExplainer: Linking Graph Neural Networks and Large Language Models for Explainable Vulnerability Detection
A review of deep learning methods for non-invasive fetal electrocardiography
Artificial neural network-based non-parametric ground motion models for multiple intensity measures in Türkiye
Cellular and subcellular specialization enables biology-constrained deep learning
PCD-GAN: A perceptual and copyright dual generative adversarial network for artwork style transfer and copyright protection
Mapping fusion zones to motion resistance: Finite element insights into return-to-activity after interbody fusion
SOS1: tracking the evolving path from promising to actionable therapeutic target in RAS-dependent cancers
Longitudinal visualization tools for advanced characterization of multiple sclerosis lesions using diffusion MRI and magnetization transfer imaging metrics
A Formal Framework for Evaluating Reasoning Integrity in Language Models
Applications of artificial intelligence in peripheral neurosurgery: a systematic review
Physiological Expression Mapping in Cardiometabolic Disease: A State-Space Framework with Clinical and Hemodynamic Evidence

methodology_note: All three proxies use OpenAlex's group_by endpoint. Country attribution follows OpenAlex's institution-to-country mapping (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2). A paper with US and Chinese co-authors is counted in both country totals. The top-conference proxy (source.type:conference) covers all major AI venues without venue-specific curation. The citation threshold filters out the long tail of low-engagement papers, making conference presence more signal-rich. Known limitations: OpenAlex undercounts Chinese domestic journals/conferences not indexed internationally; citation counts favor English-language venues.
